back_translation: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered:
'How long will you keep talking on and on? Show some understanding, and then we can talk.
Why are we counted as cattle, regarded as stupid in your sight?
You who tear yourself in your anger—should the earth be abandoned for your sake, or the rock be moved from its place?
Indeed, the lamp of the wicked is put out, and the flame of his fire does not shine.
The light in his tent grows dark, and the lamp beside him goes out.
His vigorous strides are shortened, and his own schemes make him stumble.
For his own feet lead him into a net, and he walks into its mesh.
A trap seizes his heel, and a snare grips him tightly.
A noose is hidden in the ground for him, and a trap lies on his path.
Terrors surround him on every side and chase after his every step.
His strength is gnawed away by hunger, and calamity is ready at his side.
It eats away patches of his skin; the firstborn of death devours his limbs.
He is dragged from the shelter of his tent and marched off to the king of terrors.
Fire dwells in his tent, and burning sulfur is scattered over his dwelling.
The roots beneath him dry up, and the branches above him wither away.
The memory of him perishes from the land, and he has no name left in the land.
He is driven from light into darkness and chased out of the inhabited world.
He has no offspring or descendants among his people, and no survivor in the place where he lived.
People in the west are appalled at his fate, and people in the east tremble with horror.
Surely such is the dwelling of the wicked, and this is the place of the one who does not know God.'
